channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A look at Prince Andrew's antics and scandals that have tried royal patience for decades
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew has been forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gaza famine claims face mounting scrutiny as mortality data falls far short of predictions
item.description: Experts challenge Gaza famine claims by the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification, citing insufficient mortality data and declining food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From barracks to palace: Soldiers who led military coups to become state leaders
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — Weeks of nationwide Gen Z protests in Madagascar sparked by power and water shortages escalated and led to a military coup that forced President Andry Rajoelina into exile. Army officer Col. Michael Randrianirina has been sworn in as the new leader of the Indian Ocean nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE GUMEDE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya as thousands turn out to mourn former PM Odinga
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu ahead of the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Daily struggles persist in Gaza even as ceasefire offers some respite
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Bassil Naggar can finally sleep without getting jolted awake by the sounds of Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French police arrest 4 in alleged plot targeting exiled Russian activist and Putin critic
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Police in France detained four people suspected in a plot targeting exiled Russian rights activist Vladimir Osechkin, who exposes abuses in Russian prisons, France's national anti-terror prosecution office said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran’s execution rate tops 1,000 this year as death row inmates launch hunger strike
item.description: Iranian death row prisoners call on U.N. Secretary General António Guterres to intervene as the regime executes over 1,000 people since the start of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia convicts 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges
item.description: A court in southern Russia convicted 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges Friday after a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Trump explains father's hot mic moment with Indonesian president
item.description: Eric Trump sought to clarify Thursday what President Trump and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto talked about during a hot mic moment when the commander in chief's son came up during a conversation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Thatcher centennial celebration examines how Reagan partnership guides modern US-UK relations
item.description: Experts examine how the Cold War alliance between President Ronald Reagan and Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher influences today's U.S.-U.K. relationship amid Russian President Vladimir Putin's threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe's main center-left political group expels Slovak leader's party for breaching its values
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Europe’s main center-left political group on Friday kicked out the party of Slovakian Prime Minister Robert Fico, who is accused of cozying up to Russian President Vladimir Putin and undermining the rule of law in his own country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mongolia's prime minister ousted after 4 months in ruling party feud
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Mongolia ’s parliament has voted to oust the prime minister in an unusually public power struggle within the ruling Mongolian People’s Party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King Charles III's visit to Vatican marks a historic step in path of unity between two churches
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Catholic Church and Church of England, divided for centuries over issues that now include the ordination of female priests, will take a historic step on the path to unity next week when Britain’s King Charles III and Pope Leo XIV pray together in the Sistine Chapel, officials
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military drone strike on drug 'submersible' in Caribbean leaves survivors, official confirms
item.description: U.S. military drone strike destroyed a drug-smuggling submersible in the Caribbean Thursday, leaving 2 to 3 survivors in the water, a U.S. official tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Political crisis in France eases for now as prime minister survives no-confidence vote
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s latest political crisis eased — for now — when Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu survived two consecutive no-confidence votes on Thursday, averting another government collapse and giving President Emmanuel Macron a respite before an even tougher fight over the national budget.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas captors once held ordinary jobs
item.description: Israeli hostage Tal Shoham describes increased faith and gratitude after enduring torture and starvation during 500 days held by Hamas in Gaza tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to meet Putin in Hungary in effort to end 'inglorious' war
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he intends to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Hungary in the coming weeks to discuss efforts to end the war in Ukraine. Trump made the announcement following a lengthy phone call with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump signals new trust in Erdogan, raising concerns over Turkey’s ambitions in Gaza and beyond
item.description: Turkey seeks expanded influence in Gaza through construction contracts and peacekeeping roles as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan rebuilds trust with Washington after the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO allies agree to increase weapon deliveries to Ukraine
item.description: Over half of NATO allies are now part of the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program to buy U.S. weapons for the Eastern European nation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: US blasts China's expansion of rare earth export controls
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Top U.S. officials on Wednesday blasted China's major expansion of rare earth export controls as a threat to global supply chains, but said Beijing could still change course and avoid steps by Washington to decouple from the world's second-largest economy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Lawder and Andrea Shalal, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military to oversee next phase of peace deal from coordination base in Israel
item.description: The U.S. is deploying troops to oversee the next phase of the Gaza peace deal as the world waits to see who will send personnel to stabilize the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at some world leaders who have fled uprisings
item.description: A military coup this week in Madagascar forced the president into exile, joining a list of international leaders who fled their countries or went into hiding during uprisings to avoid incarceration or retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Gumede And Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan announce ceasefire after civilians killed
item.description: ISLAMABAD/KABUL, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a "temporary ceasefire" on Wednesday, Islamabad said, after an airstrike and ground fighting sent tensions between the South Asian neighbors soaring, killing more than a dozen civilians.
item.dc:creator: By Saleem Ahmed, Mushtaq Ali and Mohammad Yunus Yawar, NewsNation

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s swipe at ‘weak’ leaders highlights the shifting global influence of Western nations
item.description: Amid Trump’s visit to Egypt and Israel to celebrate the release of hostages held by Hamas and to usher in a new era of Mideast peace, some say Western influence is waning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel frees some Gaza medical staff, but a prominent hospital chief remains imprisoned
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Under Gaza’s ceasefire deal, Israel freed dozens of doctors, nurses, paramedics and other medical personnel seized during raids on hospitals. But more than 100 remain in Israeli prisons, including Dr.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: No end in sight to government shutdown as House speaker refuses to negotiate
item.description: The shutdown entered its 14th day with the Senate returning from its holiday break, Republican House Speaker Mike Johnson refusing to call lawmakers back to Washington and President Donald Trump’s budget director vowing to keep firing federal workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas holds public executions, tightens grip on Gaza
item.description: CAIRO/JERUSALEM, Oct 14 (Reuters) - Israel restricted aid into Gaza and kept the enclave's border shut on Tuesday while re-emergent Hamas fighters demonstrated their grip by executing men in the street, darkening the outlook for U.S. President Donald Trump's plan to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Nidal al-Mughrabi and Maayan Lubell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's power worldwide has 'increased 1,000%': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Sunday marked 737 days since the hostages were taken, and all 20 hostages remaining alive were released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to Knesset: Israel has won ‘all that they can’ in military campaign
item.description: President Trump on Monday urged Israeli leaders to turn the page from warfare to peace, telling the Knesset that Israel had won “all that they can by force of arms” and expressing optimism about the region’s future.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: From a hilltop village and across Israel: Tears of joy as the living hostages are returned
item.description: LAVON, Israel (AP) — It was a day of joy and tears across Israel. Families, loved ones and friends watched as Hamas released the last living hostages held captive for over two years in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9
